FateID_sim

Yimin Zhao

2024-11-12

rm(list=ls())
library(ggplot2)
source("/Users/zhaoyimin/Desktop/Kevin/branch-point-prediction/Simulation/principle curve/generator.R")
setwd('/Users/zhaoyimin/Desktop/FateID simulation')

Sample data

para = list(c(200, 200, 200, 200), 120, 5,
            2, 50, 1/250, 1000,
            50)

names(para) = c("n_each", "d_each", "sigma",
                "k", "max_iter", "modifier", "max_val",
                "size")
cell_pop <- matrix(c(4,10, 25,100,
                     60,80, 25,100,
                     40,10, 60,80, 
                     100, 25, 60,80),
                   nrow = 4, ncol = 4, byrow = T)
#coordinate of start points and end points
gene_pop <- matrix(c(20,90, 25,100,
                     90,20, 100,25)/20, nrow = 2, ncol = 4, byrow = T)

n_each <- para[["n_each"]]
d_each <- para[["d_each"]]
sigma <- para[["sigma"]]
modifier <- para[["modifier"]]

res <- generate_natural_mat(cell_pop, gene_pop, n_each, d_each, sigma, modifier, min_val = 0, max_val = 1)
nat_mat <- res$nat_mat

pos = res$pos 
pos1 = pos[1:200]
pos2 = pos[201:400]
pos3 = pos[401:600]
pos4 = pos[601:800]

#relate position to pseudotime
#segment1 
pseudotime = c(pos1)
#segment2
pseudotime = c(pseudotime, pos2 * 0.5)
#segment3
pseudotime = c(pseudotime, pos3 * 0.5 + 0.5)
#segment4
pseudotime = c(pseudotime, 0.5 + pos4 * 0.5)

Check PCA embedding

pseudotime = read.csv('./pseudotime.csv')
dat = read.csv("./dat.csv", row.names = 1)

nat_mat = as.matrix(dat)
#run pca
pca_res <- stats::prcomp(nat_mat)

#2d plot colored by pseudotime
dat_pca = data.frame('x' = pca_res$x[,1], 'y' = pca_res$x[,2],
                     'pseudotime' = pseudotime$x)

# Create the 2D scatter plot
ggplot(dat_pca, aes(x = x, y = y, color = pseudotime)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +  # size can be adjusted based on your preference
  scale_color_viridis_c() +  # Using a viridis color scale for pseudotime
  labs(x = 'PC1', y = 'PC2', color = 'Pseudotime', title = 'PCA embedding, color by pseudotime') +  # Add axis labels and color legend
  theme_minimal()  # Use a clean, minimal theme

Save the data

#save the pseudotime
write.csv(pseudotime, file = "./pseudotime.csv", row.names = FALSE)

#save the dataframe
dat = data.frame(nat_mat)
colnames(dat) = c(paste0("gene", 1:240))
rownames(dat) = c(paste0("cell", 1:800))
write.csv(dat, file = "./dat.csv", row.names = TRUE)

Run FateID

Input: gene by cell matrix

Do clustering

set.seed(17)
kmeans_result <- kmeans(dat, centers = 8)
saveRDS(file = './kmeans_result.rds', kmeans_result)
kmeans_result = readRDS('./kmeans_result.rds')
pca_data <- data.frame('pc1' = pca_res$x[,1], 'pc2' = pca_res$x[,2],
                       'cluster' = factor(kmeans_result$cluster))

# Plot PCA results with clusters
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(pca_data, aes(x = pc1, y = pc2, color = cluster)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  labs(title = "PCA Embedding with K-means Clustering", x = "PC1", y = "PC2") +
  theme_minimal()

The kmeans result looks reasonable and we will use cluster1, 4, 8 as terminal clusters.

Run FateID

library(FateID)
#declare terminal state cluster
tar <- c(1, 7, 8)

x = t(dat)
y = kmeans_result$cluster

fb = fateBias(x, y, tar, z=NULL, minnr=5, minnrh=10, adapt=TRUE, confidence=0.75, nbfactor=5, use.dist=FALSE, seed=12345, nbtree=NULL)

Visualization of fate bias

#dr <- compdr(x, z=NULL, m=c("tsne","cmd","umap"), k=2, tsne.perplexity=30, seed=12345)
dr1 = list(list())
dr1[['pca']]$D2 = pca_res$x[,1:2]
plotFateMap(y,dr1,k=2,m="pca")

Fate bias for cluster1

plotFateMap(y,dr1,k=2,fb=fb, g="t1", m="pca")

Fate bias for cluster7

plotFateMap(y,dr1,k=2,fb=fb, g="t7", m="pca")

Fate bias for cluster8

plotFateMap(y,dr1,k=2,fb=fb, g="t8", m="pca")

Differentiation trajectories

## [337] "cell67"  "cell58"  "cell122" "cell128" "cell183" "cell65"  "cell134"
## [344] "cell140" "cell42"  "cell176" "cell72"  "cell104" "cell138" "cell186"
## [351] "cell89"  "cell196" "cell149" "cell79"  "cell40"  "cell111" "cell49" 
## [358] "cell190" "cell142" "cell34"  "cell16"  "cell30"  "cell47"  "cell21" 
## [365] "cell156" "cell76"  "cell177"

Recruitment order

#length(fb$trall) = 3696
pca_embedding = as.data.frame(pca_res$x)

dat = data.frame('pc1' = pca_embedding[fb$trall,][,1],
                 'pc2' = pca_embedding[fb$trall,][,2],
                 'cluster' = y[fb$trall])

dat.start = dat[dat$cluster %in% tar, ]
dat.pred = dat[!dat$cluster %in% tar, ]
dat.pred$order = seq(0, 1, 1/(dim(dat.pred)[1] - 1))

library(ggplot2)

ggplot() +
  # Add the first plot (grey points from dat.start)
  geom_point(data = dat.start, aes(x = pc1, y = pc2), color = "grey", size = 3) +
  
  # Add the second plot (colored points from dat.pred)
  geom_point(data = dat.pred, aes(x = pc1, y = pc2, color = order), size = 3) +
  
  # Add the color gradient for the second layer
  scale_color_gradientn(colors = c("blue", "yellow", "red"), limits = c(0, 1)) +
  
  # Customize the theme
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",           # Place the color bar on the right
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, vjust = 1, face = "bold", size = 16),  # Centered title
    plot.margin = margin(t = 10, r = 10, b = 10, l = 10)                          # Adjust margins if needed
  ) +
  
  # Add labels
  labs(
    title = "recruitment order",
    color = "Order"
  )

First iteration

dat.1st = dat.pred[1:15,]
dat.other = dat.pred[16:dim(dat.pred)[1],]

library(ggplot2)

ggplot() +
  # Add the first plot (grey points from dat.start)
  geom_point(data = dat.start, aes(x = pc1, y = pc2), color = "grey", size = 3) +
  
  # Add the third plot (colored points from dat.pred)
  geom_point(data = dat.other, aes(x = pc1, y = pc2), color = "grey", size = 3) +
  
    # Add the second plot (colored points from dat.pred)
  geom_point(data = dat.1st, aes(x = pc1, y = pc2), color = 'red', size = 3) +
  # Customize the theme
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",           # Place the color bar on the right
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, vjust = 1, face = "bold", size = 16),  # Centered title
    plot.margin = margin(t = 10, r = 10, b = 10, l = 10)                          # Adjust margins if needed
  ) +
  
  # Add labels
  labs(
    title = "1st iteration"
  )

Second iteration

dat.2nd = dat.pred[16:30,]
dat.other = dat.pred[c(1:15, 31:dim(dat.pred)[1]),]
library(ggplot2)

ggplot() +
  # Add the first plot (grey points from dat.start)
  geom_point(data = dat.start, aes(x = pc1, y = pc2), color = "grey", size = 3) +
  
  # Add the third plot (colored points from dat.pred)
  geom_point(data = dat.other, aes(x = pc1, y = pc2), color = "grey", size = 3) +
  
    # Add the second plot (colored points from dat.pred)
  geom_point(data = dat.2nd, aes(x = pc1, y = pc2), color = 'red', size = 3) +
  # Customize the theme
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",           # Place the color bar on the right
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, vjust = 1, face = "bold", size = 16),  # Centered title
    plot.margin = margin(t = 10, r = 10, b = 10, l = 10)                          # Adjust margins if needed
  ) +
  
  # Add labels
  labs(
    title = "2nd iteration"
  )
